strengths: Experience with BI tools (Power BI, Tableau)
Version control and CI/CD (Git, GitHub Actions)
weaknesses: Limited direct real-world evidence study experience
No explicit R Shiny or interactive app development
No mention of peer-reviewed publications or literature review experience
improvement_bullets: Developed automated reporting dashboards using R and Power BI to synthesize real-world evidence and communicate quantitative findings to cross-functional stakeholders.
Conducted literature reviews and data cleaning workflows to support public health research projects, applying foundational knowledge of observational study designs and bias mitigation.
Integrated Python-based data pipelines with AWS virtual environments to manage health services research datasets, ensuring data integrity for downstream analysis.
Collaborated on GitHub to develop interactive data visualizations, translating complex clinical datasets into user-friendly summaries for public health policy impact.
Refined quantitative methodologies in data programming by implementing rigorous validation protocols to ensure findings are suitable for professional reporting and publication support.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the HEOR Intern, Real-World Evidence opportunity with Precision Medicine Group. I am interested in this opportunity because of your leadership in integrating advanced analytics with life sciences to improve patient outcomes. What interests me about working for Precision Medicine Group is your commitment to leveraging real-world evidence to drive healthcare strategy; this inspires me because it mirrors my own drive to turn complex, messy data into actionable, high-impact clinical insights. I believe my proficiency in statistical programming and my experience in building robust, automated data pipelines would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on data governance and accuracy. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I developed statistical models to identify key demographic segments, effectively replacing manual reporting with an automated R and Power BI framework. This transition reduced data discrepancies by 40% and provided the statistical evidence necessary for stakeholders to optimize marketing spend based on empirical trends. Ultimately, I know that the HEOR Intern, Real-World Evidence role at Precision Medicine Group would allow me to bring my analytical rigor to support your ongoing research and protocol development. Additionally, my role as a Data Analytics Fellow at COOP Careers enabled me to develop my data storytelling and visualization skills. I cleaned and analyzed a 250K+ row dataset to visualize geographic patterns in public safety, ultimately presenting these findings to support policy-oriented decision-making. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my quantitative methodology skills in working on real-world evidence studies at Precision Medicine Group. I know that my technical proficiency in SQL and Python and my passion for health services research align with what it takes to succeed at the HEOR Intern, Real-World Evidence role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
